altText.7: Question 7. In paragraph 3, when the author says that "Neil felt like the universe was calling him," 7 she is referring to how A he heard the booming voice inside the Hayden Planetarium on his first visit B he was delighted by seeing the stars inside the Hayden Planetarium C his experiences looking at the stars made him want to learn more about astronomy D he wanted to spend more time in the countryside because he could see more of the sky
altText.8: Question 8. Paragraph 6 of the passage supports paragraph 3 by showing that Neil 8 A saw the same things at home that he saw at the planetarium B wanted to return to Pennsylvania to use his new binoculars C continued his interest in learning about the universe D tried to share his interest in stars with his parents
altText.9: Question 9. g What do paragraphs 6 through 8 show about Neil's parents? A They work hard to give Neil everything he wants. B They are supportive of Neil's interests. C They set good examples for Neil to follow. D They want Neil to become a scientist.
altText.10: Question 10. The information in the section "LEARNING ABOUT THE UNIVERSE" adds to the 10 information in the rest of the passage by showing how Neil A finally became a successful student B made new discoveries with his telescope C made choices that helped him become an astrophysicist D earned money to pay for his education as an astrophysicist
altText.11: Question 11. Which sentence best supports the main idea of the passage? A "Not long after this, Neil and his family took a trip to Pennsylvania." (paragraph 3) B "Neil lived with his parents, his older brother, and his younger sister in a tall building called the Skyview Apartments." (paragraph 4) C "In fact, his third-grade teacher wrote a note on his report card." (paragraph 5) D "He often took his telescope to the roof of his apartment to study the night sky." (paragraph 10)
altText.12: Question 12. 1 2 According to the passage, which sentence best describes Neil? A He is serious about reaching his goals. B He is tired from working different jobs. C He is proud of his success in school. D He is happy to help his family earn money.
altText.19: Question 19. g Which sentence best explains why Hayley is chosen to perform in the talent show? 1 A Other students think she is cool. B She plays the ukulele well in the tryouts. C Her act is more unusual than others'. D Her great-great aunt used to play music.
altText.20: Question 20. Why are paragraphs 4 through 7 important to the story? A They show how accepting Mr. Y is. B They describe how confused Tilly is. C They describe how worried Hayley's dad is. D They show how supportive Hayley's family is.
altText.21: Question 21. Which statement about the talent show is true? 21 A Most students like Hayley's talent show act the best. B The talent show has performers from different grades. C Some performers canceled their acts in the talent show. D Hayley knows all the other performers in the talent show.
altText.22: Question 22. 2 2 Which paragraph does the illustration best support? A paragraph 10 B paragraph 11 C paragraph 13 D paragraph 15
altText.23: Question 23. Which happens as a result of Hayley's performance in the talent show? 2 3 A Many students respect Hayley's musical ability. B Many students learn to play the ukulele. C Skeeter starts a band for ukulele players. D Hayley understands how to be a celebrity.
altText.24: Question 24. Which sentence best states a central message of the story? A Learning to do something new takes time. B Making mistakes is a part of growing up. C Conquering fear can lead to success. D Sharing with friends brings happiness.
altText.25: Question 25. 2 5 What is Kathryn's first clue that Nitro is blind? A Nitro seems to be afraid. B Nitro does not know where his food is. C Nitro runs into the fence. D Nitro does not look outside his cage.
altText.26: Question 26. Which quotation best states the main idea of the passage? A "'He would sit and stare at the concrete walls,' Kathryn said." (paragraph 3) B "That cage had been so small, Nitro had been able to memorize every inch." (paragraph 5) C "'We just don't know what caused his condition,' Kathryn said." (paragraph 7) D "They had to help a blind tiger find his way, without the use of his eyes." (paragraph 8)
altText.27: Question 27. Which detail from the passage best shows that Kathryn cares about Nitro? 2 7 A She puts Nitro into a new cage. B She does not want Nitro to hurt himself. C She thinks Nitro has a great attitude. D She does not understand why Nitro is blind.
altText.28: Question 28. Which statement from the passage shows that the problem in paragraph 8 was solved? 2 8 A "Nitro walked through his big new home." (paragraph 9) B "He was trying to feel what was ahead of him." (paragraph 9) C "Once he learned where his fences were, the people at the Rescue put down sand pathways." (paragraph 13) D "In time, Nitro learned where every bump, every tree, and every food box was in his new cage." (paragraph 15)
altText.29: Question 29. In p aragraph 16, what does the word "mastered" suggest about Nitro? 29 A that he needed a larger space B that he felt comfortable in his space C that he liked people to visit his space D that he wanted to defend his space
altText.30: Question 30. What causes Nitro to chuffle? 30 A being aware of people B feeling afraid C wanting a treat D having a cough
altText.31: Question 31. 3 l What makes Nitro "a Rescue favorite,, (paragraph 18)? A his reaction to visitors B his response to peppermint C his unusual chuffle D his large paws
